q: Vaccination: Which type of vaccine introduces weakened but live pathogen?
options: Subunit vaccine — contains only purified protein antigens from the pathogen surface
mRNA vaccine — delivers genetic instructions for the host to produce a pathogen protein
Inactivated vaccine — contains pathogen killed by heat or chemicals
Live attenuated vaccine — contains a weakened but living pathogen that can replicate but rarely causes disease
explanation: Live attenuated vaccines (e.g., MMR) use pathogens weakened so they replicate enough to stimulate strong immune memory but are too weak to cause disease in healthy individuals.
